heading: Cooperation with foreign countries on preventing evasion of trade remedy laws

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall seek to negotiate and enter into bilateral agreements with the customs authorities or other appropriate authorities of foreign countries for purposes of cooperation on preventing evasion of the trade remedy laws of the United States and the trade remedy laws of the other country.

children.label: A
children.text: On the request of the importing country, the exporting country shall provide, consistent with its laws, regulations, and procedures, production, trade, and transit documents and other information necessary to determine whether an entry or entries exported from the exporting country are subject to the importing country’s trade remedy laws.

children.label: B
children.text: On the written request of the importing country, the exporting country shall conduct a verification for purposes of enabling the importing country to make a determination described in subparagraph (A).

children.label: C
children.text: The exporting country may allow the importing country to participate in a verification described in subparagraph (B), including through a site visit.

children.label: D
children.text: If the exporting country does not allow participation of the importing country in a verification described in subparagraph (B), the importing country may take this fact into consideration in its trade enforcement and compliance assessment activities regarding the compliance of the exporting country’s exports with the importing country’s trade remedy laws.

children.heading: Consideration
children.label: b Consideration
children.text: The Commissioner is authorized to take into consideration whether a country is a signatory to a bilateral agreement described in subsection (a) or a party to the USMCA (as defined in ) and the extent to which the country is cooperating under the bilateral agreement or the USMCA, as the case may be, for purposes of trade enforcement and compliance assessment activities of U.S. Customs and Border Protection that concern evasion by such country’s exports.section 4502 of this title
the status of any ongoing negotiations of bilateral agreements described in subsection (a), including the identities of the countries involved in such negotiations;
the terms of any completed bilateral agreements described in subsection (a); and
bilateral cooperation and other activities conducted pursuant to or enabled by any completed bilateral agreements described in subsection (a).
